pages.raw_text:
That the 20 shippers representing
the Spokane Shippers association
who met last night and ANONYMOUSLY
surrendered to the railroads were not
proud of what they were about to do
10 evidenced by their debarring news
paper men from their session.


President Schiller of the shippers
association, Manager Goodall of the
Whitehouse company and one or two
a local conference and to mark those
who We're wiled into surrendering.


Crane . Close Second.


1t was warm meeting. Many
things of interest to the general pub
lie were developed. Fred Mason and
l.. NJ. Booth led the retreat. with
George Crane a close second. Presi-
dent Schiller and f. l.. Goodall tried
to stem the Bull Run tide. 1t was of
no use. Schiller spoke for half
hour using the lash numerically. A
Fictitious listener remarked:


"That Ia the way Mason and Booth
talked when the fight began. and see
them now"


Another remarked that number of
backbones were slowly crumbling.


Cutting Comment.


Mr. Flynn of Miller, Mower &
Flynn interjected:


"All kinds OF fabrics sometimes re-
quire n little starch"


But the house that Jack built
crumbled before this bit of hot air
wired from st. Paul to H. A. jackson
Of the Great Northern yesterday:


jackson: congratulate you upon
the action of the executive commit
tee of Me shippers. which l am GER
tain cobld not have been secured
without the assistance of our friends
among the shippers. BLABON."


The telegram was read to the ship
,pers at last nights meeting and was
received rs rare condescension by
some and with derision by others. 1t
developed one fact, however, that the
executive committee, which had re-
fused their action TO the newspapers
had allowed the local railroad officials
to wire their resolution to the officials
at st, Paul, who, as appeared by the
telegram, were already congratulate
ing themselves on their easy victory.
This action of the executive commit
tee was scored as without authority.


Delegation for Chicago.


The meeting finally decided to al
low a coterie of its number to ride
